======= RMA00899Albert Rich Brand papers21-18-899Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Albert Rich Brand papers
Brand, Albert Rich.
21-18-899
1.5 cubic feet. 1.5 cubic feet. 48 phonograph records.,
1928-1940. Correspondence, printed materials, publications, research materials Brand's papers relating to ornithology. Collection material in English 2086077
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
October 2013
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, October 2013
Date modified:
Jude Corina, June 2017
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Ornithologist.
Research associate, Cornell University; ornithologist at the American Museum of Natural History. Brand was born in 1889 and died in 1940.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Brand's correspondence (1928-40) with Arthur A. Allen, Peter Paul Kellogg, Herbert Sanborn of Vanderbilt University, Dr. Frank E. Lutz, curator of the Museum, and others, mainly concerning his pioneering work recording the songs and calls of American wild birds and his ornithological writing; drafts and reprints of his articles and speeches (1930-38); typescript notes (1930-39) titled "Bird Diaries" and "Bird Hearing Experiment"; typescripts and photographs (one bound volume) describing the Cornell-American Museum of Natural History Ornithological Expedition of 1935, as well as letters from Allen and Kellogg to Brand during and after the expedition; correspondence of Herbert J. Seligmann with Brand's widow, Ernestine I. Brand, and others concerning his proposed biography of Brand and drafts of the first four chapters of this book; other biographical data, including a scrapbook of clippings concerning Brand; photographs, correspondence, and printed items concerning ornithological meetings; and lists of his publications. Includes a leather-bound book entitled "Hunting with a Mike" that was issued in 1935 to the members of the Cornell sound expedition, with a photographic and text account of the trip. The collection also includes 48 phonograph recordings of birds and frogs made Brand including two put out by the American Foundation for the Blind. Also, thesis, Recording Bird Songs, 1932.
RELATED MATERIAL
The Peter Paul Kellogg papers, #21-18-893, contain recordings made by Brand.
The James Taylor Tanner papers, #21-18-1362 and the Arthur Augustus Allen papers, #21-18-1255 contain additional materials about the Laboratory of Ornithology and the 1935 Cornell-American Museum of Natural History Ornithological Expedition.
Cite As:
Albert Rich Brand papers, #21-18-899. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Phonograph records. Scrapbooks. Photographs. Biographies. Ornithologists. Scientific expeditions. Ornithology. Bird-song -- Recording and reproducing. Birds -- United States. Allen, Arthur A. (Arthur Augustus), 1885-1964. Brand, Ernestine I. Kellogg, Peter Paul, 1899-1975. Lutz, Frank Eugene, 1879-1943. Sanborn, Herbert. Seligmann, Herbert J. Cornell University -- : Faculty. American Museum of Natural History

Scope and Contents
Mainly taken on recording trips with Arthur Allen. Photos include Arthur Allen, Peter Paul Kellogg, Elsa Guerdrum Allen, Herbert Friedman, and Charles BrandNotebook of newspaper clippings1921-19402Hunting with a Mike by Albert R. Brand19352
Scope and Contents
Bound book of the Cornell/Museum of Natural History expedition. Includes photos of the people involved with the expedition and their activities, including George Miksch Sutton, George Lowery, J. J. Kuhn, and others. Also includes photos of the ivory billed wood pecker.American Ornithologists' Union, Chicago1934-10-23-1934-10-251PhotographAmerican Ornithologists' Union, 53rd Stated MeetingUndated1PhotographGroup photo (unidentified)19381Photograph50th Anniversery American Ornithologists' Union, Museum of Natural History, New York19331PhotographSeries II. Date and title are engraved in the center of the record.Kirkland's WarblerSR-5512-552219373

"To Paul with my compliments, yes compliments!"
